name: nano-banana-2
description: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input (up to 14 images), Google Search grounding. Triggers: nano banana 2, nanobanana 2, gemini 3.1 flash image, gemini 3 1 flash image preview, google image generation
capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview
Edit existing images by providing reference URLs
Combine up to 14 input images for complex generation tasks
Generate multiple image variations in a single request
Enable Google Search grounding for real-time information integration
author: inference-sh-9
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 46 patterns but most are false positives from markdown documentation. The 31 'external_commands' detections are markdown code fences, not executable code. The 7 'weak crypto' flags reference SHA-256 checksum verification in docs, which is good security practice. Two legitimate concerns: (1) pipe-to-shell install pattern at line 14, and (2) network URLs to inference.sh. The skill uses restricted Bash(infsh *) commands only. Safe for publication with user warning about CLI installation.
valueStatement: Create stunning images from text descriptions using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview. Edit existing images, combine multiple inputs, and get real-time information grounding through Google Search integration.
userTitle: Generate Images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account authentication
Image generation depends on external Google API availability
Maximum 14 input images for multi-image editing tasks
Output quality depends on prompt specificity and model capabilities

useCases.targetUser: Content marketers and social media managers
useCases.title: Marketing Content Creation
useCases.description: Generate custom images for social media posts, blog articles, and advertisements without needing a designer.

useCases.title: Product Visualization
useCases.description: Create product mockups, concept art, and design variations to visualize ideas before production.

useCases.title: Image Editing and Enhancement
useCases.description: Modify existing images by adding elements, changing styles, or improving composition through natural language instructions.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Image Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an image with this prompt: [describe your desired image with specific details about subject, style, colors, and mood]
promptTemplates.scenario: Create a simple image from a text description

promptTemplates.title: Multiple Variations
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate 4 variations of this concept: [your prompt]. Use aspect ratio 16:9 for widescreen display.
promptTemplates.scenario: Get multiple options to choose the best result

promptTemplates.title: Image Editing
promptTemplates.prompt: Edit this image [image URL]: [describe the changes you want, such as adding objects, changing colors, or modifying the scene]
promptTemplates.scenario: Modify an existing image with specific edits

promptTemplates.title: Real-Time Scene Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an artistic visualization of [current event or topic] using Google Search for real-time information. Style: [photorealistic/illustration/watercolor].
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate images based on current events or real-time data

bestPractices: Write detailed prompts including subject, style, lighting, composition, and mood for best results
Use specific aspect ratios that match your intended output platform (16:9 for videos, 1:1 for social media)
Enable Google Search grounding when you need images reflecting current events or real-time information
antiPatterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'make something cool' - specific details produce better results
Avoid requesting copyrighted characters, real people, or trademarked logos in generated images
Do not rely solely on automatic settings for critical projects - review and iterate on results

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI required for this skill?
faq.answer: Run 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' followed by 'infsh login' to authenticate. For manual installation with checksum verification, visit https://dist.inference.sh/cli/checksums.txt

faq.question: What image formats are supported for input images?
faq.answer: The skill supports JPEG, PNG, and WebP formats for input images. You can provide up to 14 images for multi-image generation tasks.

faq.question: How do I get higher resolution output images?
faq.answer: Add the 'resolution' parameter to your input with values '1K' (default), '2K', or '4K'. Higher resolutions may take longer to generate.

faq.question: Can I generate images based on current events or news?
faq.answer: Yes, enable Google Search grounding by setting 'enable_google_search' to true. This allows the model to access real-time information for more accurate and current visualizations.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are available for image generation?
faq.answer: Available aspect ratios are 1:1 (square), 16:9 (widescreen), 9:16 (portrait), 4:3 (standard), 3:4 (portrait standard), and auto (model chooses optimal ratio).

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many images I can generate at once?
faq.answer: You can generate multiple images in one request using the 'num_images' parameter. The skill also supports up to 14 input images for complex editing and combination tasks.
